questions.prompt: What does the TypeScript compiler (tsc) do with type annotations at build time?
options: Strips them entirely — only JavaScript remains
Embeds them as comments in the output
questions.explanation: TypeScript performs type erasure. All annotations, interfaces, and generics are removed. The output is plain JavaScript.

questions.prompt: Which API is available in Node.js 18+ but was browser-only before that?
questions.explanation: fetch became available natively in Node.js 18. The others (document, localStorage, navigator) remain browser-only.

questions.prompt: What command runs TypeScript type-checking without emitting any output files?
questions.explanation: tsc --noEmit performs the full type check but writes no files. Standard for CI pipelines.

questions.prompt: Name TWO APIs that are Node.js-only and not available in a browser.
questions.answer_key: Any two of: fs, path, process, http, net, child_process, os, cluster, dgram

options: Google's JavaScript engine that implements ECMAScript
questions.explanation: V8 is Google's implementation of the ECMAScript spec. It runs inside Chrome and Node.js.
